texto: When you meet an extraordinary person, it’s like they get inside you, under your ribs, and shuffle everything inside you around until they find space for greatness to grow. But extraordinary people always get away. And when they leave, they take that little part of you with them. Suddenly you find yourself with a gap in your chest that you don’t know how to live with. Suddenly you’re frightened of being yourself without them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Chloe Rattray: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “When you meet an extraordinary person, it’s like they get inside you, under your ribs, and shuffl…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, loss, extraordinary pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e loss.

texto: A week feels like a year when you’re seventeen and in love. A twenty minute drive might as well be an ocean. But we were together again and the whole world was rejoicing, even the gravel crunched melodiously under our feet as we danced onward through the night.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Chloe Rattray: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “A week feels like a year when you’re seventeen and in love. A twenty minute drive might as well b…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que felicidade, distance, happiness pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

texto: People aren’t always what you want them to be. Sometimes they disappoint you or let you down, but you have to give them a chance first. You can’t just meet someone and expect them to be everything you’re looking for and then be angry when they’re not every hope and aspiration you projected onto them. It’s foolish to believe that someone will be what you imagine them to be. And sometimes, when you give them a chance, they turn out to be better than you imagined. Different, but better.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Chloe Rattray sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “People aren’t always what you want them to be. Sometimes they disappoint you or let you down, but…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amizade, realization, second-chances, útil em momentos de realization e second-chances.

texto: This is just the way life is – sometimes cruel, sometimes beautiful, all the time confusing and frightening and exhilarating – and we just have to deal with it. That’swhat freedom is.

texto: Every person comes with a place. It can be a house, a park, a building. When you meet someone, you are unknowingly meeting a place, a two-for-one deal that neither party is really aware of yet. Regular Joe’s is Marcus’ place. Mine is the library, second floor, nonfiction, the table between the Poker aisle and European History. Two-thousand days of friendship means that we share.
explicacao: Citação de Chloe Rattray sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Every person comes with a place. It can be a house, a park, a building. When you meet someone, yo…” articula amizade, reflexao, place em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.

texto: It all comes down to this: when you recognise your loneliness in another person, when you see desperation so familiar to yours written across someone else, you can’t just let them leave.
